2606.07150.title: From Privacy to Workflow Integrity: Communication-Graph Metadata in Autonomous Agent Interoperability
2606.07150.paper_summary: Agent-interoperability protocols such as A2A and MCP standardize what agents say to one another, but assume address-based transport over HTTP(S). Such transports protect message content, increasingly with end-to-end encryption. What they leave in the clear is the communication graph: which agent contacts which, when, and how often. In agent systems this graph is more consequential than a privacy framing suggests. Endpoints are often capability-labeled, workflows are structured and chained, and interactions are coupled to real actions, so an observer recovers more than past relationships. It can infer the pending workflow, the task being assembled and the action likely to follow. At machine speed, it can act on that inference before the workflow completes. The threat is therefore one of workflow integrity, not privacy alone: predictive leverage over autonomous action. We give a threat model for the agent communication graph; identify what makes agent metadata distinctively revealing (semanticity, prospectivity, actuation); define transport- and bootstrap-layer privacy properties and weigh candidate transports (SimpleX/SMP, Tor, mixnets) against them; and present an A2A case study in which a metadata-protecting binding is expressible but surfaces the protocol's identity assumptions. We test these on a generative model anchored to a real A2A capture. From passive metadata alone, with no payloads, a classifier recovers a task's class well above chance, from only the workflow's opening; applied together, the properties drive that recovery sharply back toward chance. Beyond what an observer can recover, we measure the leverage of acting on the leak: from a workflow's opening and under a fixed budget, an adversary choosing which workflows to act on realizes in this model most of a clairvoyant attacker's advantage over a metadata-blind one, and the same properties suppress it.
2606.07150.paper_summary_zh: 代理互操作性協議如 A2A 和 MCP 標準化了代理之間的交流內容，但假設基於地址的 HTTP(S) 傳輸。這些傳輸保護消息內容，越來越多地使用端到端加密。它們留下的明文是通信圖：哪個代理在何時、以何種方式聯繫哪個代理，以及聯繫的頻率。在代理系統中，這個圖比隱私框架所暗示的更具影響力。端點通常被標記為能力，工作流程是結構化和鏈接的，互動與實際行動相耦合，因此觀察者能夠恢復的不僅僅是過去的關係。它可以推斷出待處理的工作流程、正在組裝的任務以及可能隨之而來的行動。在機器速度下，它可以在工作流程完成之前根據該推斷進行行動。因此，威脅不僅僅是隱私，而是工作流程的完整性：對自主行動的預測性杠杆。我們提供了一個代理通信圖的威脅模型；確定使代理元數據具有獨特揭示性的因素（語義性、前瞻性、啟動性）；定義傳輸層和引導層的隱私屬性，並根據這些屬性權衡候選傳輸（SimpleX/SMP、Tor、混合網絡）；並呈現一個 A2A 案例研究，其中可表達的元數據保護綁定顯示了協議的身份假設。我們在一個基於真實 A2A 捕獲的生成模型上測試這些。僅從被動的元數據，沒有有效載荷，分類器能夠從工作流程的開頭準確恢復任務的類別，遠高於隨機概率；當這些屬性一起應用時，則驅動該恢復急劇回到隨機概率。超出觀察者能夠恢復的內容，我們測量了利用洩漏進行行動的杠杆：從工作流程的開頭，在固定預算下，選擇行動的對手在這個模型中實現了大部分預知攻擊者相對於盲元數據攻擊者的優勢，而相同的屬性則抑制了這種優勢。
2606.07150.author: Bijaya Dangol et.al.
